threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Haggerston Station' instead of 'Haggerston' like the locals. It’s just Haggerston, mate.
Last night, heard two lads arguing about the best fried chicken in Brixton - one swore by Morley's, the other yelled 'cluck off!' at the mention of KFC.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It hit me when I saw a group of kids selling home-made lemonade outside Lidl, chatting about their favorite flavors like they were royalty.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can have a midnight snack at an open-air Turkish market, surrounded by graffiti murals—where else do street vendors serve döner to the sound of live DJs?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their knack for dodging the dog droppings on 24th Street, while the newcomers are too busy staring at their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 29 right as a fog rolled in, full of people sharing their weekend stories. Muni's a strange mix of the mundane and magical here.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but they miss the local bakeries where the real community gathers for morning coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still stare at the menu in cafes, waiting for someone to explain tapas. No one does—just order the pan con tomate.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you ask for a 'roll' and get a confused look instead of a bread roll. It’s a 'barm' or 'batch' around these parts, mate.
Caught a night bus last week, sat next to a bloke passionately explaining the merits of various fried chicken shops in Brixton. He swore by the one with the neon sign.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a guy bargaining with a Waschbär over a leftover kebab outside my window. That's when I knew this place has layers.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find park benches with built-in chess tables—perfect for spontaneous games between strangers, day or night.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always tell who’s from here by how they order their burrito—locals ask for a side of salsa, not extra. It's the little things.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo on the 14 Mission sharing his wedding playlist with the whole bus. Only in San Francisco do you get wedding vibes mid-commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just pop into locals' bars and get a drink. But the best tapas spots are often hidden behind the busy streets, away from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they always ask for a tall glass of 'agua con gas' at the bars instead of just ordering a 'copa de agua'.
